title: ipomoea pedicellata
intro: Ipomoea pedicellata, also known as the 'Indian potato', is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Ipomoea pedicellata include its ability to treat fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. Ipomoea pedicellata is often prepared as a herbal tea or powder, and it can also be used as a topical application for skin problems.
